flashcards.front: When should you use a Set instead of a List?
flashcards.back: Use a Set when you need to ensure uniqueness of elements and don't care about insertion order.

flashcards.front: What's the time complexity difference between HashSet.contains() and ArrayList.contains()?
flashcards.back: HashSet.contains() is O(1) while ArrayList.contains() is O(n).

flashcards.front: Why might you choose LinkedList over ArrayList?
flashcards.back: For frequent insertions/deletions at the beginning/middle of the collection, as LinkedList offers O(1) for these operations.

flashcards.front: What's the memory trade-off between ArrayList and LinkedList?
flashcards.back: ArrayList uses less memory but has slower insertions/deletions. LinkedList uses more memory (for node objects) but has faster insertions/deletions.

flashcards.front: How can you make an ArrayList thread-safe?
flashcards.back: Use Collections.synchronizedList() or CopyOnWriteArrayList for thread-safe operations.
